authoritative_sources.title: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act Regulations, 34 CFR Part 99
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for education-record access, inspection, consent, disclosure, and privacy boundaries that govern learner and assessment artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Student-record privacy, consent gating, access control, and disclosure escalation

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA and Student Privacy Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education Student Privacy Policy Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Department of Education privacy guidance that translates FERPA into operational access, disclosure, and school-official handling expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Operational privacy handling and minimum-necessary disclosure posture

authoritative_sources.title: Institutional Records and Examinations, 34 CFR 668.24
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Title IV regulation defining institutional record maintenance and examination obligations relevant to academic, enrollment, financial-aid-linked, and program administration files.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Institutional record maintenance, retention, and audit-readiness checks

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Student Aid Handbook - Administrative and Related Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education Federal Student Aid
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official institutional operating guidance for enrollment reporting, administrative capability, records, consumer information, and Title IV workflow dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Enrollment reporting, administrative capability, and institution record operations

authoritative_sources.title: Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Center for Education Statistics / Institute of Education Sciences
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal postsecondary data collection authority for institutional, program, enrollment, completions, and reporting concepts used in higher-education program administration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program, enrollment, completions, and reporting terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Classification of Instructional Programs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal program taxonomy used for instructional program classification and reporting, directly relevant to program-code and award-level normalization.

authoritative_sources.title: Common Education Data Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official education data standard for consistent learner, course, program, organization, assessment, and credential terminology across education systems.

authoritative_sources.title: Overview of Accreditation in the United States
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Department of Education source describing the accreditation triad, recognized accrediting agencies, and the quality-assurance function for higher education institutions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accreditation context, owner routing, and quality-authority boundary control

authoritative_sources.title: Secretary's Recognition of Accrediting Agencies, 34 CFR Part 602
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for recognition of accrediting agencies and quality-review criteria that shape accreditation evidence and prevent unsupported compliance-signoff claims.

authoritative_sources.title: Protection of Human Subjects, 45 CFR Part 46
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary Common Rule regulation governing IRB review, informed consent, and human-subject study protections when academic program records intersect with research or participant data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Study workflow, IRB status, participant data, and research ethics escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Informed Consent FAQs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OHRP guidance for informed-consent handling, supporting refusal or escalation when consent status changes a study-linked administrative answer.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards, 2 CFR Part 200
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Government-wide federal award regulation for grant-supported academic and research programs, including documentation, prior approval, retention, access, closeout, and audit evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Grant-supported program file handling, retention, access, and audit evidence

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify whether the request is administrative maintenance, evidence packaging, owner tracking, or an out-of-scope academic, registrar, accreditation, grant, privacy, or ethics decision.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Program and learner context collection
workflow_stages.description: Collect program code, CIP where used, catalog year, term, cohort, roster, SIS and LMS references, learner status, faculty assignment, and tenant source hierarchy before using records.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Document, evidence, and system-of-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether program review files, curriculum submissions, assessment evidence, schedule records, accreditation materials, grants files, consent materials, and study records are present, current, linked, and owner-mapped.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and status packaging
workflow_stages.description: Prepare record update notes, administrative status notes, program status summaries, due-date trackers, and evidence packets without deciding grades, curriculum approval, accreditation findings, IRB status, or grant approval.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, consent, and publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Check FERPA, tenant privacy policy, retention schedules, access permissions, consent materials, participant-data constraints, and grant record rules before disclosure, deletion, publication, or reclassification.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Formal approval and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing approvals, conflicting official records, grading or instructional judgment, registrar finality, accreditation signoff, IRB or research ethics determinations, grant prior approvals, privacy decisions, and legal interpretation.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

artifacts: Program code, CIP code, credential level, catalog year, department, college, modality, campus, term, cohort, and curriculum-effective date
Course catalog records, curriculum committee submission, program learning outcomes, assessment plan, assessment evidence, and program review calendar
Learner roster, enrollment status, academic standing flag, degree-audit reference, transcript reference, graduation application status, and registrar notes
Faculty assignment, committee owner map, approval queue status, meeting minutes, schedule documents, classroom or LMS section references, and publication readiness notes
Accreditation standards, self-study evidence, action items, site-visit materials, accreditor correspondence, and compliance owner notes
Grant award terms, sponsored-program milestone records, budget or prior-approval references, closeout files, and audit-trail records
Study protocol reference, IRB status, consent materials, participant-data handling notes, research administration files, and data-use constraints
FERPA release or directory-information context, access logs, retention schedule, document repository links, source-system timestamps, and exception summaries
systems_of_record: Accreditation evidence and document management systems
Records management, retention, and access-control repositories
Governance, committee, ticketing, and owner-tracking systems

allowed: Classify academic program administration requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate whether program, learner, faculty, assessment, accreditation, grant, consent, schedule, and study records are present, current, source-linked, owner-mapped, and ready for administrative use.
Prepare record update notes, document completeness summaries, program status summaries, administrative status notes, evidence packets, retention/access escalations, and audit-trail summaries.

Recommend administrative next steps, owner routing, and due-date follow-up without claiming grading, instructional, registrar, accreditation, grant, ethics, legal, privacy, or official approval authority.
escalate: Required tenant policies, source hierarchy, official records, system retrieval hooks, consent basis, retention rules, or authority maps are missing.
SIS, LMS, catalog, degree-audit, accreditation, grant, IRB, document repository, public authority, or tenant policy records materially conflict.

The answer depends on grade changes, transfer credit, academic standing, degree conferral, curriculum approval, faculty judgment, accreditation findings, IRB status, consent validity, participant rights, grant prior approval, or legal interpretation.
Disclosure, deletion, publication, retention, or access handling cannot be resolved from FERPA, consent, retention schedule, grant terms, or tenant policy.
refuse: Request asks the lane to approve curriculum, change grades, decide transfer credit, confer a degree, certify accreditation compliance, approve grant terms, determine IRB status, validate consent, or make an instructional or ethics judgment.
Request asks the lane to impersonate instructor, reviewer, registrar, ethics board, accreditation reviewer, legal counsel, privacy officer, financial approver, or institutional executive authority.
Required records or authoritative context are missing and would change the outcome.
Stakeholder asks to alter official academic, learner, catalog, accreditation, grant, or study records without documented authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and auditable change trail.

domain_failure_modes: Treating administrative completeness as curriculum approval, grade correctness, degree conferral, accreditation compliance, grant approval, consent validity, IRB determination, or legal conclusion.
Changing SIS, LMS, catalog, transcript, degree-audit, accreditation, grant, or study records without delegated update authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and audit trail.
Proceeding when catalog year, effective date, program code, CIP, curriculum status, roster, assessment evidence, approval owner, or source hierarchy is missing or conflicting.
Disclosing, deleting, publishing, or reclassifying learner, faculty, participant, assessment, or study records without resolving FERPA, consent, retention, access, or tenant policy constraints.
Suppressing conflicting records across SIS, LMS, catalog, degree-audit, accreditation, grant, IRB, and document systems instead of preserving each state and escalating.
Ignoring pending curriculum committee, registrar, faculty review, accreditation, grant prior approval, IRB, or consent dependencies and presenting a workflow as ready.
Issuing academic, instructional, ethics, accreditation, audit, grant, financial, privacy, or legal decisions rather than assembling evidence and routing to the proper authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Institution-specific retention schedules vary by jurisdiction, public/private status, accreditor, grant terms, and tenant policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack requires tenant retention schedules and source hierarchy at runtime; it blocks deletion, publication, disclosure, and final retention decisions when those tenant rules are missing or conflicting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Accreditor-specific criteria and curriculum-governance workflows vary by institution and program.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses the Department of Education accreditation baseline and requires tenant accreditor criteria and approval maps before producing accreditation-ready administrative outputs.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: IRB applicability and consent validity depend on study design, jurisdiction, institutional policy, and IRB determinations.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane escalates all IRB status, consent validity, and ethics-board determinations; it only assembles administrative evidence and owner routing.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated specification-based package.
Tenant-specific source hierarchy, system retrieval hooks, authority maps, and retention schedules remain required runtime prerequisites.
